speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Welcome back, [username]!Today, we're firing up something smoky, juicy, and absolutely irresistible — Paneer Tikka in TWO different flavors!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Stick with me — perfect marinade, perfect grill marks, and the perfect smoky finish. Don’t worry, this recipe is beginner-friendly!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Before we start, let’s quickly check our ingredients.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Yogurt
Ginger Garlic Paste
Kasuri Methi
Mustard Oil
Salt

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Carom Seeds
Roasted Gram Flour
Degi Mirch
Panchranga Achaar Paste
Turmeric Powder

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Green Capsicum
Onions
Red Bell Peppers
Paneer

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Let’s start with the marinade! Grab a mixing bowl — this part is super easy.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Add yogurt, ginger garlic paste, kasuri methi, and mustard oil.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Mix it well until smooth.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If the yogurt isn’t sour enough, add lemon juice. If it's already sour — don’t push your luck!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Add salt, ajwain, and roasted besan. Besan helps the marinade cling better.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Now divide this lovely marinade into two bowls.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: We’re making TWO flavors!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: For the first one, add degi mirch. Mix it gently. If it looks too thick, add a teaspoon of water.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: For the second, add achaar paste and a pinch of turmeric. Achari marinade tends to thicken — that's normal!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Add capsicum, onions, bell peppers, and paneer into BOTH marinades.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Coat well. TIP: Cold paneer breaks easily — keep it slightly chilled, not frozen!

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If a paneer cube slips away — catch it! Wasting paneer is a crime.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Time to skewer! Thread everything carefully. Stay patient and don’t poke your fingers!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Heat mustard oil on a grill pan on medium-high heat (180–200°C) until it starts smoking. Add butter.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Ah, that aroma!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Place the skewers on the pan and grill them evenly. Rotate every 1–2 minutes. Don’t press too hard or the paneer will crumble.

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: If the butter browns too fast, your heat is too high! Bring it down unless you want a burnt surprise.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Transfer the grilled tikkas to a plate.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Now for the epic part — SMOKE INFUSION!

speaker: Guard
dialogue_text: Do NOT touch the coal with bare hands unless you're secretly fireproof.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Uncover — look at that smoky magic! Sprinkle kasuri methi on top.

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Your Paneer Tikka — Classic & Achari — is ready! Serve hot with your favorite chutney!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Serve hot with your favorite chutney!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Delicious!

speaker: Chopper
dialogue_text: Keep improving konyaro!
